Hey guys, Jordan Buffle here. Time for your weekly intensive training guys. We're getting to the end of the year. Pretty exciting here. So here's let me give you the schedule. We have this week right? I'm gonna have one training next week right and then we'll get a skip. So we have only one training left this year. So you got to make these next two training counts. That's why I've kind of got some topics I think are really perfect for the last two trainings of the year and then we're gonna skip that week. It'll be new years you know in between Christmas and New Year's and then we're gonna come back as strong as ever for the new year. Alright so that's the plan just so you know. So we have again we're gonna have today's training and then we're gonna do next week. What I'm probably gonna do is I'm gonna do a special Q&A the week after so they like before Christmas. I'll figure that out here because I want to do one final Q&A before the end of the year. Alright so count the number of today right? There's the Q&A afterwards. There'll be next week the Q&A afterwards and then I'll try to sneak in one more special Q&A in the evening before we all break for Christmas. Alright and there we go. So I've got a great topic for today and it has to do with the congruence between your vision for the future and the standard that you set for yourself and those two better be in line because if they're not you have what's called incongruence or dissonance and you find yourself either in a state of angst where you you know you're working really hard and you just feel like there's more in life like you're not kind of you know you're not it's not just motivated but inspired you don't feel inspired and then conversely you might find a situation where you are you know inspired in the sense that you have this incredibly robust and bright vision for the future and you really know where you want to go in life but for some reason you can't get yourself to do the things you have to do each day to get there. They're very different things and I meet people of both varieties these people like that are sort of like the hapless dreamers in life where they have great ideas and just you know they talk about the future they're gonna be rich they're gonna build a business they're gonna just be super successful but when I see their actions day to day they just don't add up to a person that's gonna be wildly successful. There's a certain level of focus there's a certain level of work ethic a certain level of commitment that you have to have to get really fucking rich in this world there just is and the reason for that is because we live in a highly incredibly competitive global world where you have probably I don't know what the number is 10 million 100 million you know 500 million really success-oriented people who work in non-stop to try to recognize their dreams to try to get their vision for the future turned into reality so you're competing against them so if you're not putting in that same effort that same elbow grease at that same level of commitment to barrel through every obstacle and barrier that's there as you wait you're not gonna get what you want plain and simple now before we start you know think that like I have all these skill I don't have those that would be delusional no that when I talk about stretching the proper way to make this vision statement what I'm talking about is
